extras.definition: U.S. electronic network for batch credit and debit transfers between bank accounts, governed by NACHA rules.
Share of enrolled loyalty or subscription members who transacted in the last 90 days.
HL7 messaging family carrying patient movement events; backbone of EHR-to-downstream integration.
Share of card authorisation attempts that are declined for any reason.
Annualised value of active subscriptions on a normalised basis, excluding one-time fees.
Hierarchical list of components, sub-assemblies, and quantities needed to build or service a finished good.
Customer Average Interruption Duration Index — minutes off per interruption experienced.
Quality system process for investigating, correcting, and preventing recurrence of defects or non-conformances.
One minus the mean absolute percentage error of treasury cash forecasts versus actuals.
U.S. Federal Reserve supervisory exercise assessing capital adequacy of large bank holding companies.
Card disputes raised by issuers as a share of total card sales over a window.
Discounted future revenue (or margin) attributable to a customer over the relationship.
Group of patients, customers, or assets sharing a defined characteristic and tracked over time.
Ad price per thousand impressions; benchmark for digital and linear inventory monetisation.
Share of scheduled flights operated on the day of departure, net of cancellations.
Defective units detected per million received from a supplier — quality scorecard input.
Share of submitted healthcare claims rejected by payers on first adjudication.
Share of hotel bookings transacted on the brand's own channels rather than via OTAs or GDS.
Average days between invoice issuance and cash collection.
System of record for clinical patient data — orders, notes, results, encounters.
Federally mandated device that records driver hours-of-service for commercial fleets.
Reporting framework covering environmental impact, social performance, and governance practices.
Operating cash flow less capex; primary measure of corporate liquidity generation.
HL7 standard for exchanging healthcare information using RESTful resources.
Units passing all quality checks on the first attempt as a share of units started.
Total value of goods sold through a marketplace before take rate, returns, or discounts.
NCQA quality-of-care measure set widely used by U.S. health plans.
aliases: Healthcare Effectiveness Data and Information Set

extras.definition: U.S. DOT/FMCSA rules limiting driving hours for commercial motor vehicle operators.
Airline schedule disruptions including cancellations, delays, and diversions.
Global XML/JSON message standard for financial communications, replacing legacy SWIFT MT.
Cash held in non-yielding accounts above operating buffer — opportunity-cost target for treasury.
Fee paid by merchants' acquirers to card issuers for processing card transactions.
Customer identification, verification, and risk-rating program required of regulated financial institutions.
HQLA divided by 30-day net stressed cash outflows; Basel III liquidity standard.
Combined work-in-process plus AR days — key cash-tied-up metric in professional services.
4-digit ISO classification of merchants by business type, used for routing and pricing.
Discipline and platform for governing canonical reference and master entities across systems.
Mortgage Industry Standards Maintenance Organization XML formats used in origination and servicing.
Available stable funding divided by required stable funding; Basel III long-term liquidity rule.
Availability multiplied by performance multiplied by quality — composite manufacturing productivity score.
Standardised analytics-ready schema for observational healthcare data used in RWE.
aliases: Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Common Data Model

extras.definition: Customer orders shipped or received by the committed date as a share of total orders.
Third-party travel reseller (e.g. Expedia, Booking.com) that distributes hotel and air inventory.
95th-percentile end-to-end response time; common SLO benchmark for APIs and pipelines.
Share of tested die that pass parametric (electrical performance) tests.
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard governing handling of cardholder data.
Health information that identifies an individual; subject to HIPAA Privacy & Security Rules.
CMS-derived score reflecting expected medical cost based on member demographics and HCCs.
Mismatch between two records (e.g., trade vs custody) that requires investigation and adjustment.
Hours between receipt of a return and credit issuance to the customer.
Share of subscription contracts due for renewal that successfully renew.
Hotel performance metric — room revenue divided by total available room nights.
System Average Interruption Duration Index — outage minutes per customer served.
System Average Interruption Frequency Index — interruptions per customer served.
Regulatory filing made by a financial institution to report potentially illicit activity.
Share of planned production volume actually produced on the planned line and shift.
Direct (owned) plus indirect (purchased energy) greenhouse gas emissions for a reporting entity.
Dimensional modeling pattern that preserves history of attribute changes by adding rows with effective dates.
Average elapsed time between transaction authorisation and final settlement to the merchant.
CMS aggregate rating (1–5) of Medicare Advantage plan quality and member experience.
Share of transactions processed end-to-end without manual intervention.
Dynamic uplift on base price applied to balance demand and supply in real time.
Marketplace revenue divided by GMV — the platform's share of transaction value.
Share of cloud resources carrying mandatory cost-allocation and ownership tags.
Share of devices or users actively allowing collection of usage and reliability telemetry.
Days from case filing to final court disposition.
200,000 multiplied by recordable incidents divided by hours worked — OSHA safety benchmark.
Periodic reconciliation of consumed versus contracted units, often resulting in additional billing.
Approve, decline, or counter-offer outcome on a loan or insurance application.
Per-unit (customer, ride, transaction) revenue and cost analysis used to assess scaling viability.
Billable hours divided by available hours for consultants and engineers.
Average traffic carried as a share of capacity on a telecom transport or RAN element.
IAB standard XML format for delivering video ads to player clients.
Calendar days from wafer start to completion across the fab process flow.
Share of churned customers reactivated within a specified window.
Inventory of goods that have entered production but not yet been completed.
XML-based standard for tagging business and financial data; mandated for many regulatory filings.
Acute inpatient discharge followed by another inpatient admission within 30 days.
Loan or card balance more than 30 days past its scheduled payment date.
Authentication protocol layered onto card transactions to shift liability for fraud.
HIPAA standard format for electronic submission of healthcare claims to payers.
Bank or processor that contracts with merchants to accept card payments.

An atomic unit of healthcare data exchange in FHIR (Patient, Encounter, Observation, etc.). Each resource has a stable id, a versionable meta block, and a JSON or XML serialisation defined by the FHIR specification.
A FHIR container resource that groups other resources for transmission as a single unit. Bundle types include searchset (search results), transaction (atomic write), and document (clinical document).

FHIR resource that represents an interaction between a patient and a healthcare provider — inpatient stay, outpatient visit, emergency contact. Anchors clinical observations and procedures to a specific care episode.
FHIR resource for measurements and simple assertions about a patient — vital signs, lab results, social history. Components carry coded result values typed via UCUM units and LOINC codes.
FHIR resource representing an order or prescription for a medication. Carries medication coding (typically RxNorm), dosage instructions, and intent (order, plan, proposal).

ONC-defined minimum set of data classes and elements that certified EHRs must support for interoperable exchange. Versioned annually; v3 added social determinants of health and clinical notes elements.
aliases: United States Core Data for Interoperability

extras.definition: Universal coding system for laboratory and clinical observations maintained by the Regenstrief Institute. Required by USCDI for lab results and many vital sign panels.
aliases: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes

extras.definition: Normalised naming system for clinical drugs maintained by the U.S. National Library of Medicine. Provides stable concept identifiers (RXCUIs) for medication coding in FHIR MedicationRequest and prescription workflows.
U.S. clinical modification of WHO ICD-10 used for diagnosis coding on claims and clinical documentation. Roughly 70,000 codes covering disease, injury, and external cause classifications.
Comprehensive clinical terminology covering findings, procedures, and observable entities. Required by USCDI for problem list and encounter type coding; richer than ICD for clinical decision support.
aliases: Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine — Clinical Terms

extras.definition: A pointer from one FHIR resource to another, expressed as a relative URL like Patient/123 or an absolute URL. Enables the resource graph that ties Observations to Encounters to Patients.
A FHIR terminology resource enumerating the codes drawn from one or more CodeSystems that may be used in a specific binding. Used to constrain coded fields in profiles and forms.
A FHIR terminology resource that defines the concepts (codes, displays, hierarchies) of a code system such as LOINC or SNOMED CT. Distinguished from a ValueSet, which selects from CodeSystems.

FIX message that carries a single order from a buy-side firm to a broker or venue. Identifies the instrument, side, quantity, order type, and time-in-force; typically the first message in an order's life cycle.

FIX message a venue or broker sends to acknowledge, fill, partially fill, replace, cancel, or reject an order. The execution life cycle of every FIX order is told through a stream of ExecutionReports.

FIX message that delivers a full snapshot of the current state of a market data subscription — top-of-book or depth-of-book — at a point in time. Contrast with MarketDataIncrementalRefresh for tick-by-tick updates.
A numeric field identifier in a FIX message body, e.g. 35=MsgType, 55=Symbol, 38=OrderQty. Tag-value pairs are SOH-delimited; the schema is versioned by FIX protocol release (4.4, 5.0 SP2, FIXT).
FIX message used to report a trade between counterparties to a venue or central matching service. Drives downstream affirmation, allocation, and settlement workflows.

An XML representation of an OTC derivative trade — IRS, CDS, FX option — defined by ISDA's FpML standard. Carries economic terms in product-specific blocks and is widely used in SEF and DTCC reporting.
The post-trade process of splitting a block execution across the underlying client accounts, typically expressed via FIX AllocationInstruction (J) and AllocationReport (AS) messages.

A logically continuous, sequenced bidirectional conversation between two FIX endpoints, identified by SenderCompID/TargetCompID. Sessions handle Logon, Logout, Heartbeat, ResendRequest, and SequenceReset administrative traffic.
A read-only, real-time FIX feed of executions sent to a third party — typically a clearing broker, risk system, or compliance group — for shadow position keeping and surveillance.

A standardised schema and vocabulary that lets researchers run identical analyses across heterogeneous data sources. OMOP, PCORnet, and Sentinel are the dominant CDMs in observational health research.
A row in the OMOP CONCEPT table representing a single clinical idea — a drug, condition, procedure, or measurement — identified by a stable concept_id and tied to a Vocabulary and Domain.
An OMOP-defined clinical area (Drug, Condition, Procedure, Measurement, Observation, Device) that determines which CDM table a Concept belongs in. Domain-routing is the first step in ETL into OMOP.
A coded terminology managed within OMOP — SNOMED, RxNorm, LOINC, ICD-10-CM, NDC, etc. The Vocabulary tables define concept ids, hierarchies, and concept-to-concept relationships.

The canonical concept that OMOP-standardised analytics build on — typically SNOMED for conditions, RxNorm for drugs, LOINC for measurements. Source codes are mapped to a Standard Concept via concept_relationship.
The original code from the source system (ICD-9-CM, NDC, local lab codes) preserved alongside the Standard Concept after ETL. Lets researchers audit and re-map without losing fidelity.
A reusable specification of a patient population — entry events, inclusion criteria, exit rules — typically authored in ATLAS and exported as a JSON definition or generated SQL.
OHDSI's open-source web application for browsing OMOP vocabularies, designing cohort definitions, characterising populations, and generating analytic SQL against a CDM database.

Federated observational studies executed by sites in the Observational Health Data Sciences and Informatics network, each running the same analytic package against their local OMOP CDM and contributing only aggregate results.
An OMOP CDM concept representing a span of time during which a person's clinical events would have been recorded. Critical for denominator construction in incidence and prevalence studies.

CDISC standard for organising the raw clinical trial data submitted to FDA and PMDA. Each trial domain (DM, AE, LB, EX) maps to a defined SDTM table with prescribed variables and controlled terminology.

CDISC standard for the analysis-ready datasets that support a clinical trial's statistical analysis plan. Built from SDTM with traceability variables; ADSL (subject-level) is the foundation for all efficacy and safety ADaM datasets.

CDISC machine-readable metadata document submitted alongside SDTM/ADaM data. Describes datasets, variables, controlled terminology, and value-level metadata so reviewers can validate the submission programmatically.
CDISC XML standard for capturing and exchanging clinical trial case report form data and metadata between EDC systems, sponsors, and regulators. Preserves audit trails and electronic signatures.
ICH-maintained terminology for adverse event coding in pharmacovigilance and clinical trials. Five-level hierarchy from System Organ Class down to Lowest Level Term.
The MedDRA hierarchy level used as the unit of analysis for safety reporting. AEs reported by sites at the verbatim level are mapped to a Preferred Term during medical coding.
SDTM variable carrying a study-wide unique subject identifier, typically formed by concatenating study, site, and subject id. The join key for almost every cross-domain SDTM and ADaM operation.

Legacy fixed-position binary file format that FDA and PMDA require for SDTM and ADaM submission. Variable names are limited to 8 characters and labels to 40 characters, which constrains CDISC variable naming.
JSON object an exchange sends to bidders inviting them to bid on a single impression opportunity. Carries imp, site/app, device, user, and regs blocks per the OpenRTB specification.
The minimum CPM a publisher will accept for an impression, set per imp in the OpenRTB BidRequest. Hard floors filter bids below the threshold; soft floors are advisory in second-price auctions.

A publisher-side technique that solicits bids from multiple SSPs in parallel before the ad server's waterfall, increasing competition for each impression. Implemented client-side via Prebid.js or server-side via PBS.
Programmatic auction model in which each impression is sold individually via a sub-100ms auction across many bidders. The dominant transaction shape on most open exchanges.

Pre-negotiated programmatic deals between specific buyers and sellers, executed over the OpenRTB rails but with deal_id-scoped pricing and inventory. Includes Preferred Deals, PMPs, and Programmatic Guaranteed.

An IAB Tech Lab plaintext file at /ads.txt on a publisher's domain listing the sellers and seller account ids authorised to sell its inventory. Buyers reject bids from unauthorised sellers to combat domain spoofing.
Companion to ads.txt — a JSON file an SSP hosts at /sellers.json declaring every seller it represents. Lets buyers verify the supply chain end-to-end via the SupplyChain Object in OpenRTB.

IAB Tech Lab specification for in-app and in-web ad measurement that lets a single SDK report viewability and verification signals to many measurement vendors without each requiring its own integration.
Share of served impressions that meet the MRC viewability standard — at least 50% of pixels on screen for at least one second for display, two seconds for video.

Ad traffic that does not represent real user interest. General IVT (GIVT) is filtered with declared bot lists; Sophisticated IVT (SIVT) requires behavioural detection and includes hijacked devices and bot farms.

IAB Europe framework for collecting and signalling user consent under GDPR, encoded in a Consent String passed through the OpenRTB User Ext. v2.2 added explicit purpose disclosures and cleaner legitimate-interest handling.

IAB Tech Lab successor signalling format that combines TCF, U.S. State, and other regional consent strings into a single header-bearable string. Designed to scale to new privacy jurisdictions without re-plumbing.

IAB Tech Lab object model that factors out shared types (placement, creative, context) used by OpenRTB, OpenDirect, and adCOM consumers. Versioned independently of OpenRTB to allow separate evolution.

Open Charge Alliance protocol between EV chargers (Charge Points) and their backend (Central System / CSMS). Versions 1.6 (SOAP/JSON) and 2.0.1 (JSON over WebSocket with security profiles) are the deployed baselines.

EV roaming protocol between charge point operators (CPOs) and e-mobility service providers (EMSPs). Standardises Locations, Tariffs, Sessions, and CDR exchange so a driver can roam across networks with a single subscription.
Pricing schedule a charging session is billed against — combining time, energy, and parking components, often with time-of-use bands. Carried in OCPI as a Tariff object referenced from each session.

ISO standard for communication between an EV and a charging station, including high-level protocol stacks for AC and DC charging. Underpins Plug-and-Charge identification and bi-directional energy transfer.
ISO 15118 capability that lets a driver authorise and pay for a charging session purely by plugging in — the vehicle and charger exchange certificates without a card tap or app interaction.
Bi-directional charging that allows an EV battery to discharge to the grid during peak demand under a utility's V2G program. Requires ISO 15118 -20 and bi-directional onboard or DC chargers.
OCPP message a charge point sends periodically during a transaction reporting energy.active.import.register, current, voltage, and other sampled values. The audit trail behind every kWh billed.

OCPP 1.6 message (TransactionEvent in 2.0.1) a charge point sends when a session begins, carrying connectorId, idTag, meterStart, and timestamp. Pairs with StopTransaction at session end to bound a billable charging session.

Card or fob a driver presents at a charge point to authenticate a charging session in lieu of an app or Plug-and-Charge. Identified by an idTag string sent in OCPP Authorize and StartTransaction messages.

FOCUS column representing the cost a cloud provider invoices for a charge in the billing period, after commitment-based discounts and credits are applied. The number that reconciles to the customer's invoice.

FOCUS column representing the amortised cost of a charge after spreading commitment fees across the term they cover. Used for unit-economics comparisons that need consistent treatment across reservation purchases.
FOCUS column representing the cost of a charge at the publicly listed on-demand price, before commitment, negotiated, or volume discounts. Useful for measuring discount yield versus list.
A pricing construct in which a customer commits to a baseline of usage in exchange for a discount versus on-demand. FOCUS represents these via CommitmentDiscountId, Type, and Status columns.
A key-value label attached to a cloud resource for cost allocation, ownership, or environment tracking. FOCUS surfaces tags via the Tags column as a JSON-encoded map for analytic querying.
FOCUS controlled-vocabulary value that buckets each charge into a high-level cloud service group (Compute, Storage, AI and Machine Learning, Networking, etc.) for cross-provider comparison.
FOCUS column expressing the unit a charge is priced in — vCPU-Hours, GB-Months, Requests. Lets analytics aggregate quantities across providers without conflating heterogeneous units.
FOCUS pair of columns (ChargePeriodStart, ChargePeriodEnd) bounding the time window a charge applies to. Foundational for accurate amortisation and commitment-coverage analytics.

ISO 20022 securities settlement instruction message used by an account owner to instruct its custodian on a delivery or receipt of securities. Carries trade details, settlement parties, and SD/SD+1 instructions.
ISO 20022 securities post-trade message a custodian sends to report transaction postings on a securities account. Anchors break investigations and reconciliation against trade-side records.
Field on a securities settlement instruction reporting whether the CSD has paired the instruction with the counterparty's matching instruction. Unmatched and mismatched are the two failure states.
12-character ISO 6166 identifier for a security — country code, NSIN, and check digit. The dominant cross-border instrument identifier used in settlement, custody, and regulatory reporting.
ISO 9362 8- or 11-character code identifying a financial institution and optionally a branch. Used to address SWIFT MT and ISO 20022 messages and to identify settlement parties in CSD instructions.

ISO 17442 20-character identifier for a legal entity participating in financial transactions. Mandated by EMIR, MiFID II, and Dodd-Frank for transaction-level reporting; issued by Local Operating Units.

ISO 10962 6-character code classifying an instrument by category, group, and attributes. Required alongside ISIN for MiFID II transaction reporting and helps disambiguate equity classes and bond seniority.

EU regulation that governs CSDs and securities settlement, including the Settlement Discipline Regime that imposes cash penalties for late or failed settlements and mandates buy-ins for persistent fails.
Settlement cycle in which trades settle on the business day following the trade date. The U.S., Canada, and Mexico moved to T+1 in May 2024; the EU and UK have committed to follow in October 2027.
A CSDR-mandated procedure in which a failing seller's broker is forced to source the securities in the open market to deliver to the buyer, with the original seller covering any price difference.

A lender's instruction calling back loaned securities — typically because the underlying owner has sold or wishes to vote a corporate action. Failure to return on time can trigger a buy-in under CSDR.

IRS XML-based platform for electronic filing of federal tax returns. Replaced the legacy EMS system; supports business and individual returns with real-time acknowledgements and structured error feedback.
The U.S. individual federal income tax return. Schedules attach to address specific income types, deductions, and credits beyond the main return.
The federal income tax return for U.S. C corporations, including subsidiary forms 1120-S (S corporations), 1120-F (foreign corporations), and 1120-L (life insurance companies).
The federal return for partnerships and LLCs taxed as partnerships. Schedule K-1 flows through to each partner's individual or entity return.

Supplemental form that attaches to a base IRS return to detail a category of income, deduction, or credit (e.g. Schedule A itemised deductions, Schedule C self-employment, Schedule K-1 partner income).

An MeF response message confirming the IRS's receipt of an e-filed return and reporting whether it was Accepted or Rejected with a list of validation errors. Logged into MeF audit trails per submission.
U.S. statute requiring foreign financial institutions to report on accounts held by U.S. persons, enforced via withholding on non-compliant payments. Reported on Forms 8966, 1042, and 1042-S.

OECD standard for automatic exchange of financial account information between participating jurisdictions. Functions as the international counterpart to FATCA and is implemented via local CRS reporting forms.

Mechanism by which a payer remits a portion of a payment directly to a tax authority on behalf of the payee. U.S. examples include W-2 wage withholding, 1042 nonresident withholding, and backup withholding.

Gross income minus above-the-line adjustments (educator expenses, HSA contributions, student loan interest, etc.). Drives eligibility for many tax credits, IRA deduction limits, and Medicare premium tiers.

Parallel federal tax computation that disallows certain preferences, deductions, and credits. Filers pay the higher of regular tax and AMT; corporate AMT was reintroduced under the 2022 IRA at a 15% book-income floor.
System-generated, source-independent integer or hash key used as the primary identifier of a dimension or fact row. Decouples the warehouse from upstream identifier changes and supports SCD2 history.
The identifier a business uses for an entity in its source system — a customer number, ISIN, NPI. Stored alongside the surrogate key but never used as the join key in the dimensional model.

Data Vault 2.0 modeling pattern. Hubs hold business keys, Links capture relationships between Hubs, and Satellites carry descriptive attributes with effective dates, enabling auditable, parallel-loadable warehouses.
A hash of a row's descriptive attributes used to detect change cheaply during ETL. In Data Vault Satellites, a new row is inserted only when the hash differs from the most recent active row.

A dimension shared across multiple fact tables with identical structure and meaning, enabling drill-across analysis. Conformed Date, Customer, and Product dimensions are the backbone of bus-architecture warehouses.

A table that resolves many-to-many relationships between a fact and a dimension — for example, a household membership bridge between an account fact and a customer dimension. Often carries a weighting factor for fair allocation.
The exact level of detail a fact row represents — one transaction, one daily snapshot, one claim line. Defining the grain is the first step in dimensional design and should never silently change.

A dimension row that arrives after a fact references it. Handled by inserting a placeholder dimension row at fact-load time and back-filling the descriptive attributes when the dimension feed catches up.

Open protocol (Google + Visa + Mastercard + Amex et al.) that defines IntentMandate, CartMandate, and PaymentMandate as signed payloads carrying agent-initiated purchase authority through the payments stack. Includes carry-back of disputes to agent operators.
JSON-RPC 2.0 contract published by Anthropic for connecting LLM agents to external tools, resources, and prompts. Implemented by Stripe, Shopify, Square, PayPal MCP servers among others.

Network-issued identifier (Visa Trusted Agent Protocol / Mastercard Agent Pay) that uniquely identifies a transacting agent across operators. Carried in the auth message via the Visa AGI / Mastercard AAI flag.
The signed chain of authority from principal → operator → agent → merchant that proves an agent's transaction was legitimately authorized. Verified at AP2 PaymentMandate evaluation time.
Signed declaration of what the principal wants the agent to accomplish (budget, deadline, category). The grain at which agentic-conversion attribution is computed.
Out-of-band re-authentication of the human principal via WebAuthn or W3C Secure Payment Confirmation when an agent transaction crosses a risk-threshold (amount, new merchant, anomalous geography).
A single JSON-RPC 2.0 method invocation an agent sends to an MCP server (e.g. search_products, create_cart, payment_intent.create). Recorded for trace, billing, and audit.
A purchase whose attribution chain terminates at an agent's IntentMandate rather than a click on a marketing surface. Multi-touch credit assignment must operate at intent grain.
The signed transfer of a built CartMandate from agent to principal for one-tap confirmation, or from one agent to another agent (cross-platform).
Cryptographic binding of a tokenized payment instrument to a specific agent + grant + merchant tuple, so a stolen token cannot be replayed in a different context.
OAuth 2.0 extension (RFC 9396) that lets an authorization request carry structured authorization_details (amount caps, merchant scope, expiry). The protocol substrate AP2 IntentMandates ride on.
Composite trust index for an agent derived from operator attestation, behavioral history, dispute rate, and cross-network signals (Skyfire, Visa KYA, Mastercard AAI).
A non-buying agent on the other side of an agent-to-agent interaction — typically a merchant-side concierge or negotiator. Both sides require KYA and signed delegation.
Human user on whose behalf an agent acts. Remains the cardholder of record for all dispute and refund purposes.
An agent that holds a delegation token by virtue of possession (analogous to a bearer instrument). Risky relative to attested agents and typically capped at low amounts.
AP2 payload signed by the principal expressing what they want done. First node in the AP2 mandate chain.
AP2 payload built by the agent and presented to the principal for one-tap confirmation. Contains line items, totals, merchant — signed for non-repudiation.
AP2 payload that authorizes the actual debit. References the CartMandate and IntentMandate; its signature is the substrate networks check before authorization.
